scheme_name: Scheme for Rearing Purebreed Gir/ Kankarage Male Cow Calves with Excellent Genetic Quality
page_title: Scheme for Rearing Purebreed Gir/ Kankarage Male Cow Calves with Excellent Genetic Quality

details: The “Scheme for Rearing Purebreed Gir/ Kankarage Male Cow Calves with Excellent Genetic Quality” was launched by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat to encourage the rearing of Gir and Kankrej male cow calves of superior genetic quality. The primary objective is to ensure the availability of high-quality breeding bulls for pure breeding of indigenous cows across Gujarat.
benefits: Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Constituent Benefits
Scheme for rearing purebreed Gir / Kankarage male cow calves with excellent genetic quality

The institution will be assisted upto 50% of the total cost with a maximum of ₹10,000/- for the maintenance of per calf in the first year and a maximum of ₹15,000/- per year for the maintenance of per calf in the second and third years.
eligibility: Gaushalas must be registered under the Charity Act.
The institutions should have all the facilities for rearing calves.
The institutions where calves are reared will be selected by the Gauseva and Gaucher Vikas Board.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at I-Khedut Portal:
On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Animal Husbandry Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button and an application page will open.
Click on "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a print out of the online application and submit it to the board within 30 days of application with the signature and stamp of the
of the concerned district with the necessary documents listed in the application.
Applicant can also check their Application Status/View/Print/Upload Application details from the below link:

documents_required: Registration certificate of Gaushala (under Public Charity).
Proof of infrastructure/facilities for calf rearing.
Bank account details of the institution.
Land ownership or lease proof (if applicable).

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of the scheme is to promote the pure breeding of indigenous cows by supporting the rearing of purebred Gir and Kankrej male cow calves with high genetic quality.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Only Gaushalas registered under the Public Charity Act and selected by the Gauseva and Gaucher Vikas Board are eligible to apply for this scheme.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides up to 50% of the total maintenance cost of each calf- ₹10,000/- per calf in the first year and ₹15,000/- per calf per year for the second and third years.

faqs.question: Can this scheme be availed for breeds other than Gir and Kankrej?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is specifically intended for the rearing of male calves from the Gir and Kankrej breeds only.

faqs.question: Who selects the institutions for receiving the benefits of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The Gauseva and Gaucher Vikas Board is responsible for selecting the eligible institutions for receiving assistance under this scheme.

faqs.question: Where should the application for this scheme be submitted?
faqs.answer: The application must be submitted online through the iKhedut portal. A printed copy of the application should also be submitted to the concerned district’s GGVB nodal officer within 30 days.

faqs.question: What is the timeline for submitting the printed application after online submission?
faqs.answer: The printed application must be submitted within 30 days of completing the online application.

faqs.question: Which documents are required to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Required documents include the Gaushala’s registration certificate, proof of infrastructure for calf rearing, Aadhaar of the responsible authority, bank account details, land ownership or lease documents, and other documents as specified.

faqs.question: Where can an institution find more information or get help with the application?
faqs.answer: More information is available on the iKhedut portal, and assistance can also be obtained from the district’s GGVB nodal officer .
